part_title: Section Nineteen: Emergency Scenarios and Management of IPM Failure
part_short_title: Emergency Scenarios and Management of IPM Failure
part_subtitle: Emergency Scenarios and Management of IPM Failure

items.text: Integrated management programmes for Tuta absoluta do not always proceed as planned. Trap catches may rise suddenly, a wave of moths may enter from an old field, advanced larval instars may appear despite completed treatments, or fruit infestation may increase close to harvest.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Integrated management programmes for Tuta absoluta do not always proceed as planned. Trap catches may rise suddenly, a wave of moths may enter from an old field, advanced [[term:larval-instar]]larval instars[[/term]] may appear despite completed treatments, or fruit infestation may increase close to harvest.

items.text: Not every sudden increase means the programme has collapsed, and survival of larvae after treatment does not necessarily mean that the pest has become resistant to the insecticide.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Not every sudden increase means the programme has collapsed, and survival of larvae after treatment does not necessarily mean that the pest has become resistant to the insecticide.
[[prepared]]Possible causes include:

items: Continued hatching of eggs laid before treatment.
Application against a relatively insensitive larval instar.
Failure to use the correct rate.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Continued hatching of eggs laid before treatment.
[[prepared]]Application against a relatively insensitive larval instar.

items.text: The danger of an unscientific response lies in the engineer immediately resorting to:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The danger of an unscientific response lies in the engineer immediately resorting to:

items: Treating the whole field when infestation is confined to a hotspot.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Treating the whole field when infestation is confined to a hotspot.

items.text: Such responses may increase resistance, kill natural enemies, and raise residues without addressing the true cause.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Such responses may increase resistance, kill [[term:biological-control]]natural enemies[[/term]], and raise residues without addressing the true cause.

items.text: Emergency management therefore depends on three rules:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Emergency management therefore depends on three rules:

items: 2. Contain the risk source before repeating treatment.
3. Evaluate economic viability before continuing costly interventions.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]2. Contain the risk source before repeating treatment.
[[prepared]]3. Evaluate economic viability before continuing costly interventions.

items.text: An emergency is an acute or unexpected change in pest status or programme performance that requires a response faster than the routine decision cycle.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]An emergency is an acute or unexpected change in pest status or programme performance that requires a response faster than the routine decision cycle.
[[prepared]]It may include:

items: A sharp increase in trap catch.
Appearance of dense egg populations over a short period.
Entry of an immigration wave from a neighbouring field.
Inability to comply with harvest timing or the preharvest interval.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Appearance of dense egg populations over a short period.
[[prepared]]Entry of an immigration wave from a neighbouring field.
[[prepared]]Inability to comply with harvest timing or the preharvest interval.

items.text: Not every high reading should be classified as an emergency. It should also pose a risk to:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Not every high reading should be classified as an emergency. It should also pose a risk to:

items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]An increase becomes an emergency only after confirming:

items: Active infestation or imminent risk is present.
The time available for intervention is limited.

items.text: The term “outbreak” should not be used on the basis of:

11. Prevent the field from becoming a regional source.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]9. Collect evidence before changing the programme.

items.text: An emergency plan should be prepared before a problem occurs and should include:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]An emergency plan should be prepared before a problem occurs and should include:

items: A team with clearly defined responsibilities.
A sector map of the field.
Access to a laboratory capable of conducting susceptibility tests.
Equipment for collecting and destroying infested fruits.
A communication channel with neighbouring fields.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Access to a laboratory capable of conducting susceptibility tests.
[[prepared]]Equipment for collecting and destroying infested fruits.

items.text: An outbreak cannot be managed efficiently if the team begins searching for information only after infestation rises.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]An outbreak cannot be managed efficiently if the team begins searching for information only after infestation rises.

items.text: Depending on project size, the team may include:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Depending on project size, the team may include:
[[prepared]]Responsibilities should be defined:

prepared_items: [[prepared]]Who stops use of the suspected insecticide?

items.text: Conflicting decisions should be prevented during emergencies.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Conflicting decisions should be prevented during emergencies.
[[prepared]]Follow this sequence.

items: Confirm that the damage is caused by Tuta.
Confirm that the mines are active.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Confirm that the damage is caused by Tuta.

items: At a time appropriate to the development rate of the stage.

items.text: A common error is applying an emergency spray before collecting information, which prevents later diagnosis of the cause.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A common error is applying an emergency spray before collecting information, which prevents later diagnosis of the cause.

items.text: When Eggs and Mines Are Absent

items: Do not apply a general spray.
Identify the direction from which moths arrived.
Read traps daily or at shorter intervals when risk is high.

items.text: When Several Larval Instars Are Present
This indicates that the problem is not only recent immigration; an internal cycle is already established, and the decision level should be escalated.

Assuming all moths originated inside the field.
Ignoring eggs because mines are absent.

items.text: Immigration Wave from an Old Tomato Field

items: Increased catches in traps facing the old field.
More eggs on the field edge.
Lower infestation in the field interior.
An increase coinciding with cessation of irrigation, harvesting, or shredding.
Presence of heavily infested old plants.
Moth movement when plants are disturbed.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Increased catches in traps facing the old field.
[[prepared]]An increase coinciding with cessation of irrigation, harvesting, or shredding.

items: 1. Determine the timing of operations in the old field.
2. Notify the manager of the source field.
3. Increase traps and inspection along the edge.
6. Time Bt or another early-stage agent to hatching.
7. Remove host plants between the two fields.

items.text: Appearance of the First Infested Fruits
Why Is This a Turning Point?
Fruit infestation means the pest has moved from vegetative damage that the plant can partly compensate for to direct loss of marketable or processable yield.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Fruit infestation means the pest has moved from vegetative damage that the plant can partly compensate for to direct loss of marketable or processable yield.

items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This leads to unnecessary spraying.

items.text: Error Nine: Failing to Document Weather

items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Treatment interpretation becomes difficult.

items.text: Error Ten: Failing to Collect Live Samples

items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This prevents susceptibility testing.

items.text: Error Eleven: Killing Natural Enemies During an Emergency

items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This creates a later resurgence.

items.text: This turns the field into a regional source.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This turns the field into a regional source.

items.text: This allows it to spread to other fields.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This allows it to spread to other fields.

items.text: Error Fourteen: Repeating Treatment Without an Assessment Date

items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Its efficacy cannot be determined.

items.text: Error Fifteen: Treating Every Emergency as an Insecticide Problem
The problem may originate in the nursery, residues, or harvest management.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The problem may originate in the nursery, residues, or harvest management.

items: 1. Establish a standardized protocol for investigating insecticide failure.
2. Train engineers to determine larval-instar distribution.
4. Use a standardized method for Tuta absoluta.
5. Link failure cases to the IRAC record.
6. Notify neighbouring farms of immigration waves.
7. Prevent dose increases and unregistered mixtures.
8. Trace batch numbers of suspected products.
11. Establish a register of source fields.
13. Establish a plan for removing neglected fields.
14. Include quality and residue officers in emergency decisions.
15. Develop an economic decision process for advancing harvest or terminating a sector.
16. Protect natural enemies during corrective intervention.
18. Conduct a written review after every case.
19. Share confirmed resistance cases among regions.
20. Convert emergency experience into updates to the decision matrix.

items.text: A sudden increase in Tuta absoluta does not mean that the solution is to increase the number of sprays, and survival of larvae after treatment does not prove that resistance has developed.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A sudden increase in Tuta absoluta does not mean that the solution is to increase the number of sprays, and survival of larvae after treatment does not prove that resistance has developed.
[[prepared]]Emergency management begins with:

items.text: Immigration waves from old fields are among the most important causes of sudden increases, and they cannot be managed sustainably by treating the recipient field alone.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Immigration waves from old fields are among the most important causes of sudden increases, and they cannot be managed sustainably by treating the recipient field alone.

items.text: Eggs appearing along field edges provide an opportunity to intervene before damage occurs, while L1 and L2 represent the best window for larval control.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Eggs appearing along field edges provide an opportunity to intervene before damage occurs, while L1 and L2 represent the best window for larval control.

items.text: Dominance of L3 and L4 indicates delayed monitoring, poor timing, or poor coverage and requires protection of the remaining crop together with review of the causes of failure.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Dominance of L3 and L4 indicates delayed monitoring, poor timing, or poor coverage and requires protection of the remaining crop together with review of the causes of failure.

items.text: Appearance of the first infested fruit is an escalation point at which infestation percentage should be measured, infested fruits collected, and harvest timing and the preharvest interval reviewed.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Appearance of the first infested fruit is an escalation point at which infestation percentage should be measured, infested fruits collected, and harvest timing and the preharvest interval reviewed.

items.text: When larvae persist after insecticide application, review:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]When larvae persist after insecticide application, review:

items.text: Resistance should not be declared until these factors have been excluded and an appropriate susceptibility test has been conducted.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Resistance should not be declared until these factors have been excluded and an appropriate susceptibility test has been conducted.

items.text: When resistance is suspected, stop repeating the mode-of-action group, do not increase the dose, and use different alternatives within an integrated programme.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]When resistance is suspected, stop repeating the mode-of-action group, do not increase the dose, and use different alternatives within an integrated programme.

items.text: Collapse of natural enemies may also directly cause pest resurgence. They should therefore be protected and re-established rather than completely replaced by an intensive chemical programme.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Collapse of natural enemies may also directly cause pest resurgence. They should therefore be protected and re-established rather than completely replaced by an intensive chemical programme.

items.text: During an advanced outbreak, continuing control should not become an objective in itself. Advancing harvest or terminating an uneconomic sector may be the most efficient decision.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]During an advanced outbreak, continuing control should not become an objective in itself. Advancing harvest or terminating an uneconomic sector may be the most efficient decision.

items.text: Emergency management succeeds when every failure case becomes information that improves:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Emergency management succeeds when every failure case becomes information that improves:
